rationale: In the citing PDF, this citation appears in core framing sections (introduction, theory, and general discussion), not just as a passing mention. The authors explicitly position prior redemption research including Stourm et al. as focused on fixed exchange rates, which defines the gap their studies address. They also use Stourm et al. for substantive claims about the psychological value of points and consumers' reluctance to spend them, supporting their mechanism and interpretation.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Stourm, Bradlow, and Fader (2015) as key prior work on loyalty-point redemption and stockpiling in fixed exchange-rate programs. It uses that conceptual and empirical base to motivate its new analysis of how variable versus fixed exchange-rate stability changes redemption behavior.
